evidence: The word 'bran-new' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10), as it directly refers to the first appearance of something. It also scores highly in scale.individual (10), reflecting its common use for singular items. Epistemological.perception (10) and identification (10) highlight direct sensory awareness and categorization of newness. Experience.discovery (10) and creation (10) align with the atom's 'discovered' and 'made' aspects. Symmetry.generative (10) underscores the creation of something new, opposing its symmetric pair PRP.old.

Brand-new strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10) as it denotes the first appearance of something. It also aligns with symmetry.generative (10) as it signifies the creation of something new. Experience.discovery (10) reflects encountering the novel. Ontological.material (8) and informational (8) reflect the applicability to physical and abstract entities. Epistemological.identification (7) and semantic (7) relate to categorizing and labeling as new. Aesthetic value (7) comes from the appreciation of novelty.

The word 'caller' (as an adjective) strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (5) as it implies a new entity initiating action. It scores high in interconnection.catalytic (5) and symmetry.transformative/generative (5 each) due to its role in triggering processes or creating new outputs. Ontological.informational (4) and relational (4) reflect its structural role in systems like code or networks. Experience.discovery (5) aligns with encountering novel 'callers' in contexts like technology. Value.functional (3) acknowledges practical utility in systems design.

Crisp strongly resonates with material existence (physical freshness) and aesthetic value (sensory appeal of newness). It ties to emergence (new appearance), perception (sensory detection of freshness), and superficial resonance (surface-level newness). Scores drop where connections are indirect or require stretching context.

The word 'fresh' strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newness), sensory perception (e.g., fresh air), discovery (encountering the new), and aesthetic value (beauty in novelty). It also aligns with material ontology (physical freshness), generative symmetry (producing new things), and informational layers (new data). Scores drop where connections are abstract or contradictory to PRP.new's focus on recentness.

The term 'fresh-cut' strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newly created state) and ontological material (physical freshness). It connects to perception/experience through sensory immediacy and symmetry.generative (newness). Aesthetic value arises from sensory appeal, while functional utility relates to practical freshness. Scores reflect its direct association with recent creation and tangible novelty.

Freshness strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (newness as first appearance) and ontological.material (physical freshness like food). It aligns with epistemological.experience (sensed freshness) and value_generation.aesthetic (appreciation of newness). The symmetry.generative score reflects freshness as a catalyst for new possibilities, while experience.discovery highlights its link to encountering the novel.

The highest scores reflect 'good' as a value judgment (ontological.semantic), the experience of encountering new things (experience.discovery), transformative potential (symmetry.transformative), and practical utility (value.functional). Lower scores relate to how 'good' is perceived, identified, or tied to emergent phenomena. Most slots score 0 as 'good' in this context doesn't strongly align with existential, cosmic, or structural aspects.

The word 'hot' in the context of PRP.new (newness) strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (5) as it implies a recent emergence. It scores highly in interconnection.catalytic (3) and symmetry.generative (3) due to its role in triggering trends and creating new cultural phenomena. The aesthetic (3) and functional (2) value slots reflect its association with popularity and utility in new contexts. Scores are low elsewhere due to the focus on novelty and surface-level trends.

The word 'inexperienced' strongly resonates with individual scale (10), epistemological experience (10), and ontological semantic meaning (10). Weak connections include catalyzing change (interconnection.catalytic), essential resonance, contingent lawfulness (dependent on experience level), discovery experiences, and functional value (e.g., fresh perspectives). The symmetric destructive aspect reflects the negative connotation of inexperience.

The word strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (newness implies first appearance) and experience.discovery (core of encountering the new). It scores highly in symmetry.generative (producing new things) and lawfulness.emergent (unexpected emergence). Lower scores reflect direct connections to naming (epistemological.identification), material/informational layers (ontological), and independent/catalytic interconnections.

The word 'late' (as PRP.new) strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newness implies first appearance), generative symmetry (creating new things), and epistemological creation (generating knowledge). Moderate scores reflect its influence on change, informational ontology, and essential resonance as core to newness. Lower scores align with its contrast to permanence and avoidance of over-scoring.

Lateness in the context of PRP.new (recentness) strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (6) as it marks the emergence of new things. It also aligns with ontological.semantic (4) due to its core meaning of newness. Scores for experience.discovery (4) reflect the encounter with the new, while symmetry.generative (3) highlights its role in producing novelty.

The word 'new' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10) as it defines the first appearance of something. symmetry.generative (10) captures its essence of producing novelty. experience.discovery (10) and experience.creation (10) reflect the direct experiential connection to encountering or bringing new things into existence. Lower scores reflect weaker but real ties to identification (naming new things), perception (sensing newness), and value aspects like functionality/aesthetics.

The word 'new-made' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10) as it denotes first appearance. It aligns with symmetry.generative (10) as it signifies creation of something new. High scores in experience.discovery (10) and experience.creation (10) reflect the novelty aspect. Lower scores reflect its focus on emergence over other aspects like transformation or ethical value.

The word 'new-sprung' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10), symmetry.generative (10), and experience.discovery (10), as it directly denotes something newly come into existence. It also connects to epistemological.creation (7) and resonance.essential (7), reflecting the core essence of novelty and its role in generating knowledge or value. Scores for scale.individual (2) and lawfulness.emergent (8) reflect its applicability to singular instances and unexpected emergence.

The word 'newborn' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10) as it marks the first appearance of life. It scores 10 in symmetry.generative, reflecting creation of something new, and 10 in experience.creation for the act of bringing life into being. High scores in material (10), perception (8), and aesthetic (7) align with the physical, sensory, and value aspects of new existence. Lower scores reflect dependencies on context (e.g., catalytic change in families) or indirect connections (e.g., relational ties).

The highest scores reflect 'newfound's core resonance with emergence (8), discovery (10), generative symmetry (7), and knowledge creation (6). Lower scores acknowledge related aspects like individual scale (3) or informational layers (5), but most slots remain 0 as the word focuses on novelty and its immediate implications.

Newness strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10) as it defines first appearance. symmetry.generative (10) captures its essence of producing something new. experience.discovery (10) reflects encountering the unknown. High scores also align with catalytic interconnection (8), semantic layer (9), and emergent lawfulness (10), core to the concept of 'newness.'

The highest scores reflect 'novel' as fundamentally tied to emergence (10), creation of knowledge (10), semantic meaning (10), and existential essence (10). It catalyzes transformative change (8) and resonates existentially (10). Lower scores relate to its contingent nature (3), informational basis (7), and functional utility (5).

Parvenu strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (newly acquired status), scale.individual (focus on a single entity), ontological.semantic (value judgments about social standing), epistemological.identification (labeling as 'newly risen'), and symmetry.destructive (negative connotation of unearned status). Resonance.superficial reflects its surface-level critique of social pretension.

Parvenue resonates strongly with temporal.emergence (newly arrived status), scale.individual (focus on the person), epistemological.identification (labeling as 'new'), ontological.relational (social positioning), resonance.superficial (perceived as lacking depth), symmetry.transformative (social metamorphosis), lawfulness.contingent (dependent on social conditions), and value_generation.ethical (judgment on social worth). Most slots remain 0 due to the narrow scope of PRP.new.

The word 'past' resonates most strongly with the semantic slots tied to foundational contrasts between newness and its antecedents. The **ontological.semantic (3)** reflects the core meaning opposition between new and old. **Resonance.essential (3)** captures the deep contrast in essence between the two. **Symmetry.generative (3)** highlights how the past enables the new. Lower scores (2) reflect secondary connections like the past's influence on transformation, continuity, and foundational layers (material, structural, informational), as well as epistemological acts of identification and creation.

The word 'radical' in the context of PRP.new (recently made/discovered) strongly resonates with emergence (newness), transformation (fundamental change), catalytic interconnection (triggering change), generative symmetry (producing new things), and discovery/creation experiences. It also aligns with identification/creation of knowledge, informational/semantic layers, and essential resonance (core essence of novelty). Lower scores reflect its lack of ties to permanence, established structures, or moral/transcendent value.

The word 'raw' strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newness), material ontology (physical unprocessed state), and discovery experience (encountering the new). It also connects to generative symmetry (potential to create new things) and superficial resonance (surface-level, unrefined quality).

Rawness strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newness), material ontology (physical unprocessed states), and epistemological perception/experience (direct sensory engagement). It catalyzes transformation (symmetry.generative/transformative) and touches essential/existential resonance (core essence of newness). Aesthetic value arises from its unrefined beauty.

Recency strongly resonates with temporal emergence (new things first appearing), experience of discovery (encountering the unknown), and symmetry aspects like transformative/generative (new things causing change and creating new entities). It also aligns with epistemological identification (naming as new) and creation (generating knowledge), ontological informational/semantic layers (data and meaning of newness), and superficial resonance (surface-level newness).

The word 'recent' strongly resonates with temporal emergence (7), symmetry.generative (8), and experience.discovery (8) because it directly marks the timing of newness and its connection to creation/discovery. Scores for epistemological creation (4), ontological semantic (5), and lawfulness (predictable/emergent) reflect its role in knowledge generation and temporal predictability. Lower scores reflect tangential or indirect connections.

Recentness strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (7) as it marks the first appearance of new things. It aligns with ontological.informational (8) because newness often relates to data or knowledge. Symmetry.generative (8) reflects its role in producing novel entities. High scores in experience.discovery (7) and experience.creation (6) highlight its connection to encountering and making new things. Resonance.essential (7) captures its core role in defining newness.

The term 'red-hot' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (8) as it implies something newly emerged. It also aligns with symmetry.generative (7) due to its association with creating something new. Lower scores reflect its surface-level popularity (resonance.superficial 5) and dependency on conditions (lawfulness.contingent 2).

The word 'refreshing' strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newness), individual scale (personal experience), sensory perception, semantic meaning, essential resonance, generative symmetry (newness creating novelty), emergent lawfulness (unexpectedness), discovery (encountering the new), and aesthetic value (sensory pleasure from novelty). Scores are low or zero elsewhere due to lack of direct conceptual alignment with PRP.new's 'recently made/discovered' context.

The highest scores reflect 'revolutionary's core ties to emergence (new beginnings), societal-scale change, semantic/structural transformation, and the essential resonance of metamorphosis. Creation/experience and ethical dimensions highlight its role in generating new realities and moral stakes.

Rising as PRP.new emphasizes emergence (temporal) and generative potential (symmetry), with strong ties to material/structural ontology and catalytic interconnections. It resonates deeply with discovery and creation experiences, and holds functional/aesthetic value.

The term 'spic-and-span' strongly resonates with newness (temporal.emergence, symmetry.generative) and its aesthetic appeal (value.aesthetic). It connects to individual novelty (scale.individual) and the superficial, transient nature of newness (resonance.superficial). It also ties to discovery (experience.discovery) and predictable patterns (lawfulness.predictable). Lower scores reflect its lack of deep existential or structural resonance.

The word 'spick-and-span' strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newness as first appearance) and ontological material (physical newness). It scores highly in perception (direct sensory freshness), generative symmetry (producing newness), and aesthetic value (beauty of newness). Lower scores reflect its focus on surface-level newness rather than deeper existential or structural aspects.

Stale resonates most with transformation over time (temporal.transformation), physical decay (ontological.material), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and predictable decline (lawfulness.predictable). It contrasts with PRP.new by emphasizing the opposite of freshness, aligning with PRP.old's semantic pull.

The word 'strong' in the context of PRP.new (recently made/discovered) resonates most with temporal.emergence (7) and symmetry.generative (7), as new things often emerge powerfully and generate novel outcomes. Scores also reflect catalytic influence (7) and material/semantic ontological layers (7/5), emphasizing tangible and conceptual strength in new entities. Lower scores align with the contrast to PRP.old and the focus on novelty rather than permanence or systemic scale.

Sunrise as 'new' strongly resonates with emergence, transformation, and cyclical patterns. Its daily predictability and generative symbolism align with PRP.new's 'recent' connotation. Aesthetic and sacred qualities highlight its transcendent value, while stellar scale and material ontology ground it in tangible reality.

Undecomposed resonates with temporal emergence (newness), epistemological perception/identification (whole-state awareness vs. categorization challenges), ontological structural integrity, and experience of discovery. Scores reflect its focus on unaltered, novel states without deeper existential or transformative implications.

The word 'unexampled' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (first appearance of something unprecedented), lawfulness.emergent (arising unexpectedly without prior example), and experience.discovery (encountering the unknown). Lower scores reflect its core essence (resonance.essential) and generative novelty (symmetry.generative), tied to its semantic uniqueness (ontological.semantic) and identification as new (epistemological.identification).

Unprecedented strongly resonates with temporal emergence (newness) and lawfulness.emergent (unexpectedness). It ties to discovery/experience and generative symmetry (new creation). Moderate scores reflect its connection to identification/understanding (naming newness) and semantic ontology (meaning of novelty).

The word 'unspoiled' resonates most strongly with temporal.emergence (newness unaltered), ontological.material (physical purity), experience.discovery (encountering untouched newness), and value.aesthetic/sacred (beauty and transcendence of preservation). Lower scores reflect its focus on unaltered states (vs. PRP.old) and minimal connections to systemic or abstract layers.

Unspoilt resonates with identification (naming its state), material/semantic layers (physical and meaning value), independence (existing without change), generative symmetry (producing new purity), contingent lawfulness (dependent on conditions), discovery experience (finding untouched newness), and aesthetic/ethical/sacred values (beauty, morality, transcendence).

Untested resonates most with temporal.emergence (newness), epistemological.identification (labeling as unproven), ontological.informational/semantic (data and meaning of uncertainty), resonance.superficial/essential (surface-level uncertainty vs core essence of novelty), symmetry.generative (potential to produce results), lawfulness.contingent/emergent (outcomes depend on testing), and experience.discovery (encountering the unknown).

The word 'untried' strongly resonates with 'experience.discovery' (5) as it implies encountering the unknown. It also connects to 'ontological.semantic' (3) and 'resonance.essential' (3) due to its core meaning of lacking testing. Scores like 'epistemological.identification' (2) and 'symmetry.generative' (3) reflect categorization and potential for new outcomes when tested.

Unused in the PRP.new context resonates with temporal.emergence (3) as newly made items are unused until engaged. It strongly aligns with scale.individual (5) and ontological.material (5) due to its focus on tangible, discrete entities. Perception (2) and identification (3) reflect direct awareness of unused status, while experience (3) ties to lived encounters with newness. Resonance.superficial (5) captures the transient, surface-level state of unused items, and symmetry.generative (3) hints at potential new uses. Lawfulness.contingent (3) underscores dependency on conditions for use, and functional value (3) highlights latent utility.

The word 'virgin' strongly resonates with temporal.emergence (10) and experience.discovery (10), emphasizing first appearance and exploration of the unknown. It also aligns with symmetry.generative (5) as it implies creation of something new. Lower scores reflect tangential connections like material (5) for physical untouchedness and aesthetic (3) for beauty in novelty.

The word 'warm' resonates most with the emergence and influence of new ideas or objects, their individual/community impact, sensory perception, and aesthetic value. It also touches on inclusivity, generation of new things, and the superficial yet essential resonance of warmth in new experiences.

The highest scores reflect 'young' as newly emerged (temporal.emergence), generative of new things (symmetry.generative), tied to discovery (experience.discovery), and essential to newness (resonance.essential). Lower scores relate to its influence over time (temporal.influence), cause-effect chains (interconnection.chained), and transient aspects (resonance.superficial).
